title: Update Icon on Personal Injury GBP Service Card
description:

What to update:

- Replace the existing injured arm icon on the Personal Injury GBP service card with a crutches icon that better represents the service across all GBP locations.
- No icon asset provided by client — description only: "a simple icon of a person on crutches"

---
Thanks much

title: 🔴 Complete SEO content block insertion and testing for all 27 location pages
description:
WHAT: Add missing SEO content blocks to all 27 location pages using the shared design template, test form functionality, and prepare for live launch with URL redirection via 301 redirects.

WHY: Publishing velocity is critical to avoid SEO stalls and maximize search engine signals. Meg confirmed a 3-position rank gain on key terms, and delays will compromise momentum. Google's crawl rate depends on steady publishing velocity. Failure to implement 301 redirects will cause Google to split authority across duplicate URLs.

CONTEXT: Beverly Hills page already launched with new design. Christopher's team committed to finishing content blocks within one hour post-meeting. The new layout is shared across all pages, so adding SEO content blocks is the main remaining step. Contingency plan includes rolling back to draft status if issues arise.

DEADLINE: By tomorrow (2026-02-26)

DEPENDENCIES: Christopher Heckmann's team for content insertion; Clayton Coombs for SEO structure verification

⚠️ RECURRING ISSUE: Location page publishing has been discussed across all three meetings. The 9 priority pages from 2/19 meeting were to be published first, now expanded to full 27-page launch. Consistent delays due to competing priorities (award season, paid campaign relaunches) noted by Christopher.

Owner: Christopher Heckmann

Client: J399 Wilshire Law Firm — Auto-generated from Basecamp thread monitoring.

title: Add Clixsy Universal Pixel
description:
After a successful pilot, we're now looking at doing a first wave rollout for our Clixsy Universal Pixel across several client websites. Wilshire has been selected as part of the first wave rollout.

Please can we implement the pixel using HFCM.
In addition, we need to identify and add the client's GA4 ID to the tracking pixel's configuration within the Client Portal.

Default settings apply.

Please and thank you.

CC:

title: Remove "Expert" from H1s Sitewide
description:
Seeking help w/ the client request below please. I was thinking at 1st to assign it to the DEV team to do a simple search and remove but SEO might have some other steps that needs to be done. 

Summary: The client has flagged that the word "Expert" appears to have been recently added to H1 headlines across the site and is requesting it be removed. A sample page was provided showing the H1 "Expert Class Action Lawyer in California." The client is requesting a sitewide sweep to remove "expert" from all H1 tags.

Client Goal: Remove all instances of "expert" from H1 headlines sitewide, restoring headlines to their prior form without that term.

Requirements for Completion:
  • The word "expert" (case-insensitive) is removed from all H1 tags sitewide
  • H1 content is otherwise unchanged — no other words, formatting, or structure is altered
  • All affected pages are confirmed live and rendering correctly post-update
  • Affected pages are checked to ensure the update has not caused any readability issues

Assets and References:
  • Client-provided sample screenshot showing H1: "Expert Class Action Lawyer in California"
 
---


Thanks much!

title: Location Page Footer ready for review
description:
Hi, guys!

Hratch has added the locations "footer" that Clayton requested:

https://wilshirelawfirm.com/los-angeles/


A few notes:
  • He did not add links; we'll need to add these ourselves
  • These only appear on the location pages, not sitewide
Hratch's notes:

A couple of quick updates. Location pages are published and all the redirects in place for the old location pages. As soon as we have the Oakland copy we will also publish that location then proceed to purge the old location post type from the site.

The locations section of the layout has been updated to allow raw HTML, which means you can input your links with attributes directly into the custom fields. To get to the custom fields you edit the page using the default WordPress editor(not DIVI) and you can find the custom fields below the content section. Let me know if you encounter any issues adding the links to the blocks.

And a few concerns from me:
  • It sounds like they still haven't removed the old location pages, they're just redirected. Is this okay?
  • The real footer, that appears sitewide, still has links directing to the old location pages. I'd imagine this is bad...

title: High-Severity Organic Growth Plan: Timing + Feasibility Input Needed
description:
Context:


Christopher Heckmann (Wilshire Law Firm) sent over the high-severity organic growth strategy report discussed during the 5/26/26 Wilshire/Clixsy Weekly Sync. The report was authored client-side and proposes an 85-page content build across Wilshire's 8 California markets, targeting catastrophic injury, wrongful death, TBI, paralysis, and sexual abuse case types.


Per the action items from the 5/26 sync, Christopher was tasked with sharing a detailed plan with Clayton and a summarized version with Meg Choi for coordinated implementation. This email is his delivery of that plan. Meg has asked for feedback on two specific questions before any work moves forward.


Key Questions / Clarifications Needed:

  1. Content production timing (for Clayton): Given the scope, how long would Clixsy's SEO/content team realistically need to produce all content? Are there phases or priorities you'd recommend (e.g., Section A optimizations first, then B, then C)?
  2. Performance timeline (for Clayton/Mubeen): Once content is published, what's the realistic window for Wilshire to expect meaningful organic visibility gains? The report notes 2–6 months for new pages to rank.
  3. GBP name update (for ): The report recommends updating all 8 GBP listings to include "Personal Injury Lawyers" in the listing name. This is flagged as a potential quick-win. Is this something Clixsy should be driving, and are there any risks to flag before recommending it to the client?

Relevant Client Message (verbatim):

Hi team, I'm sending over the high severity organic growth strategy report we discussed on Tuesday, Clayton. Our goal is to increase organic visibility for catastrophic injury, wrongful death, and sexual abuse cases. Meg has asked for your feedback and insight on approximate timing for how long it would take to 1) produce all the content and 2) see performance gains. Please let me know if you have any questions. Best, Chris

title: Rotating Review Link Plugin Update
description:

Once we get the thumbs up, we'll loop back with Hratch and give him the green light to add it to the site.
